title: serraria
intro: Serraria is a genus of flowering plants in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the desert-holly or California heliotrope, which is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The serraria plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin and wounds. The health benefits of serraria include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and relieving pain, making it a valuable remedy for various skin conditions and injuries. In herbal preparations, serraria is often used in the form of extracts, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to affected areas for maximum benefit.
